text:
Another notable fact is that financial and high-tech companies are also
involved in the construction of the SCS (Figure 1), where private sector actors are
already collecting data from consumers to build their own credit rating services
(Table 1). It is worth noting that these parallel experiments in the marketplace
serve as an important vehicle for the state to imagine, test, and transfer potentially
viable and useful features for future inclusion into the government-run SCS. As discussed, big data–enabled surveillance aims to collect everything about
everyone (Andrejevic & Gates, 2014), and the Chinese government is advancing
 Liang et al.: China’s Social Credit System as State Surveillance                   429
